sous_titres.text: So he was only redheaded in a manner of speaking.
He didn't even have arms or legs.
There is no knowing who he was.
If they don't come, we'll freeze to death this time.
- Leave the engine running. - I know!
- I want the window seat. - That's my seat.
I always sit on the right.
- It's my seat, Hamid. - Let me sit there.
You had the window seat last time.
You make me drive, while you sit by the window,
- What does that have to do with anything? - Everything!
You want to stand here and freeze, or come and get a warm lunch?
When we get to the other side of the mountain,
the sun will shine over a beautiful, quiet village.
With a pier where people can watch the seagulls and boats.
And we can all stay there, without ever having to run again.
We were just going for a walk.
Look at you! You could at least have dressed warmer.
Idiots, what did I tell you?
I told them to dress warmly, but they didn't listen.
You two are going to pack and fly home. At least there you won't freeze.
- We aren't leaving without Hamid. - Hamid has to stay here.
He has to wait his turn.
- What's the difference between 17 and 18? - You can drink wine.
Those are the rules. I don't make them.
Sign here, and you'll get some cash.
You can take 23 kilos of luggage, tops.
23 kilos? How are we supposed to fit everything in our suitcase?
You're like a father and mother to me.
I can't be anywhere without you.
Here is the military base, and here is the center.
We'll gather the kids behind the center, then wait at the gate where Ingvild is.
We could go hang out with the girls.
They know we're being sent out of the country and that we'll break up.
I'm planning on breaking up with Ingvild.
I'll never break up with a girl.
When I saw your eyes, I lost myself in their heavenly blue.
I floated between the moon and stars, in a cradle of white clouds.
Suddenly I fell into your soft arms.
But no matter how much I love you, our journey ends here.
Love is eternal for some, but for us it is merely a dream.
Soon it will be spring, and I will be taken from you.
But to me you will always be a rose from the garden of Adonis.
Hamid, this is a poem, not a letter.
Do you want me to write more?
I need to think about it.
- Thank you so much. - Here you go.
I have some money for you.
Should I give the letter to Ingvild?
- Are you ready? - We're still waiting.
Hamid, go back to your friends.
- You don't look like them. - I copied the TV commercial.
They're sending us out of the country.
- I'm also going to the border. - Are you going to war?
No one knows when they might come.
I didn't have time to read it.
I'm not your support person anymore.
I think it's best if we break up.
I have a gift for you.
A flat iron. I thought you could have it.
It doesn't fit it in my suitcase. We're only allowed 23 kilos.
Should we give each other a goodbye hug?
that I am not allowed to respond to intimacy such as hugs, touching,
I can't take you for walks, to the movies or out for coffee.
Or listen to your personal stories, the way we used to.
That's what they said about the flat iron in the TV commercial.
Give me the flat iron back.
- You just gave it to me. - Give it back.
I'd rather give it to someone who loves me.
- Can I have some more iced tea? - Sure.
Genghis wants to tell you something. A secret.
Amel is going to run away on his birthday.
Do you see a tower here?
Or a fence, or barbed wire?
Why do you think that is?
We're in a desert. There's nothing to see around here but snow.
Should we just sit on our asses then, eat all day and get fat?
Amel may not say much, but at least he's doing something.
No one has made it before.
He will show us the way.
- How many girls are coming? - Pay up.
- 5 kroner more. - If we pay extra, do we get a guarantee?
- A guarantee? - Idiot! You think we're selling fridges?
Hey, boys, give the ball back.
Amel, don't you want to buy a ticket?
I can get you in for free.
Hamid, come back with the cash.
Hamid, what are you doing here?
This is a big day. "A happy day!"
Now you can do whatever, wish for whatever you want.
Once you get home, you can.
- You can get a girlfriend. - Or be a contestant on Paradise Hotel!
We have arranged everything. You can bring 23 kilos in your suitcase.
You can fit everything in here.
I don't want to go home.
When I spoke, they didn't believe me. So I stopped speaking.
I'll find my own way out. I refuse to be like a caged animal.
Animals know how to get to the other side of the mountain. We don't.
He has the first four digits.
- Know what kind of cake you'll get? - Same as everyone else.
Maybe they can make one without them.
I had a dream last night.
I was the only one left here. The police were chasing me through the snow.
Then I saw a donkey standing under a tree.
I ran toward the donkey, but suddenly it was gone.
The police searched for me but they never found me, so they left.
Then I poked my head out of the donkey's butt and yelled:
"Here I am, come and get me!"
That we're stuck in a shithole and will never get out of here.
Want to look at the northern lights?
There aren't any northern lights during the day.
There are always northern lights. Our eyes just can't see them.
Don't go too far! I'm watching you!
Anyone who knew his real name, country or address should tell me now.
So I can pack his belongings in a suitcase
and send them back to his family.
He could have flown home and gotten warm food.
And even a glass of wine.
Now we have to bury him here instead.
- I told him not to go up the mountain. - He wouldn't listen to us.
There's nothing out there for you.
Did you forget to take your medicine again?
He's dead. We have to forget him.
I'm thinking about the money. And the 23 kilos.
Why didn't Amel have any clothes on?
They wanted us to see him.
She needs to settle down before she can be put on the plane.
Why are you bringing her here?
She bit the other kids in the waiting hall.
Out here, we know she won't get far.
- Where is she being sent? - She has given us two identities.
- She could be either. - Family contact?
If we let her go, she's your responsibility.
I thought about it, but the ones they give us are so damn itchy.
Is it better to freeze, or itch?
- Do you have long underwear? - Of course.
Do you prefer itching or freezing?
- They've all turned 18. - How long will this take?
- Right. - How long does it take to boil an egg?
Where did she go? Where is she?
They went through the gate. Can you see them?
No, if you go through that gate, you disappear.
I think we have a new guest who needs a room.
- Come with me. - I speak Norwegian, for fuck's sake!
Then come with me, for fuck's sake!
The girls are in their rooms.
- And the boys? - In the boys' unit.
That separates the boys' unit from the girls' unit.
You can hang out together all day.
But after 8 PM, you go back to your rooms.
don't try running away. It's no use.
If you get frustrated, go down to the gym and punch it out on the sandbags.
Have a banana. You could use it.
They've closed the road to the mountain.
What are we going to do?
In the tunnel we have to whisper, as the walls can hear us.
Hamid has followed them to their training base.
If we can get to the other side, we're free.
You have to talk to Ingvild.
I can't. I broke up with her.
If you have a name, a country, and have turned 18, go to the right.
If you don't have papers, or lied about your age or nationality,
and don't have a name, just a number, go to the left.
You in the middle, who have names, countries, and are under 18 ...
Behave. And find something to keep yourselves busy.
- What? - Follow girls you don't know?
I don't even know your name.
You'll never be able to do it.
Want to come eat snow with me?
Aras says snow is full of bacteria, urine and chemicals that melt, became clouds,
and rain down on us from the sky.
Birgit told me you like poetry.
Your friends are all alike, but you're kind of weird.
Are you looking at my belly?
Hamid, what are you doing with that?
- I want to give it to the new girl. - I can take it.
I want to give it to her myself. Let me call her.
It's late, Hamid. I can't give you her phone number now.
The night overslept again, and the day was speechless.
The night overslept again, and the day was speechless.
The night overslept again, and the day was speechless.
The sun was cold, and the moon hot.
You can't fall in love with a girl who's being sent home.
I can almost smell you, you pimp.
Wait till I get my hands on you.
I'm in a place where you can't get to me, so go to hell.
No, I can't give you the password.
I think the reason you joined the army
is that you're afraid of love.
What do you know about that?
You gained enough confidence to join the army, and then you dumped me.
You told me we weren't together.
All the boys still think we are.
Stop asking me to talk to girls about serious issues. It breaks my heart.
We have to keep an eye on her and be firm with her.
I'm not who you think I am.
I'm called Aida here, but my name is also Aina.
While on the run, I was stupid enough to tell some idiot all my secrets.
She took my name, my age,
Everything I once was disappeared with that whore
I got a new name, and people started calling me Aina.
And had to run to the next country.
Is that why your hair looks so strange?
In the evening, I'm whoever I want to be.
You have to break up with her.
Who would have thought we'd have a child in a place like this?
You think it's a boy or a girl?
They told me some bad men captured you, but that they set you free.
There was a job I couldn't do.
Everything I've done, I've done halfway.
Told me to read something for them. Then they gave me a vest.
They looked kind and talked about paradise.
They wanted to meet me there.
On the way, I had to use the bathroom. We went to a house of God.
It was hot, so I took the vest off.
We can be together if you want.
I need to talk to you.
Hamid, there's nothing I can do for your case.
You're not 18 yet, so you have to stay here a while longer.
Maybe tomorrow or the day after. I don't know yet.
You're more experienced than I am.
Use your poetry, Hamid. Girls like that.
Birgit says I can't fall in love with someone who's being sent home.
- What does Birgit know about love? - I don't know anything either.
It's not you Hamid, it's love. And there's nothing you can do about it.
For every low, there's a high.
I haven't seen anything but lows when it comes to understanding love.
They didn't touch the ground, so they're probably fresh.
Do you want to come up?
Here's the center. We have to get behind it.
We'll go through the woods, here.
Ingvild will be waiting there. She has the password.
I don't want to be a potato.
Who are these two potatoes? Birgit and Sharif?
You just ate Birgit and Sharif!
Hamid, how are we going to make an escape plan now?
- We can eat your potatoes. - Yes, we can.
I don't want to talk about it.
If we give the potatoes to Aras and Ismael, they might leave us alone.
- The potatoes will solve the problem? - Maybe.
Can't you see we're in the middle of a meeting?
I can help you, if you let me join you.
You got caught by the police.
With a fence, guards and dogs.
I got bored. Some boys were bothering me.
I beat the crap out of them.
Hurry. I only have a few tickets left. It's going to be a great show today.
We didn't see anything last time.
Just some steam and girls talking.
You'll see that new girl today.
- 5 kroner. - Why do we have to pay extra?
Idiot, what's five extra to see some new pussy?
She showers completely naked and has a great body. Just wait and see.
Stop it! You can't do that anymore.
What are you doing in here?
Do you want to shower with us girls?
- I just wanted to stop ... - Girls?
What do we do with someone who sneaks into our showers?
Further up, Hamid! Are you scared?
Can you see all the way home from up there?
You're going to take us there, Hamid.
Hamid, get down! It's dangerous up there!
What if he never wakes up?
Then at least he'd be allowed to stay in the country.
He would want to be buried in a warmer place.
Idiot, you went through the ice.
It's best if you don't see her for a while.
- I want to leave. - You can't.
What about that beautiful village behind the mountain you talked about?
Was that just something you read about?
Next time you want to hurt yourself, let me know. I'll be there for you.
In the white birds' nest we listen to words.
the lion listens to his hunters before he eats them.
Want to take a walk on the ice?
You'd look good in a suit.
My grandfather liked to wear a suit.
He and his brother were so poor, they had to share the same blazer.
When one of them wore the blazer to go out,
the other one had to stay home.
I want a big wedding where we invite everyone.
We can do that. But we're fine like this too.
We can have lots of kids, maybe nine?
I promise I'll never leave you.
You're the only one who can show us the way.
You have to break up with him. It's best for you and us.
You're just using Hamid to get away from here.
You're 18 and will be deported soon.
You have to break up with him.
- That's how it's done. - You showed her.
He isn't sick, he's just different!
I spoke to the earth and it showed me the rocks.

His poems make more sense now.
If you help us, you'll never see us again.
Even if I gave you the password you wouldn't get in.
It's secret. And changes with every changing of the guard.
He says it's beautiful behind the mountains.
There's a town by the sea.
You were there, singing for me.
It was like a new beginning.
We don't have to say goodbye.
Take your happiness with you. We'll deal with the sorrow.
A broken heart is full of pain, there's no cure for that.
- Nothing. - Get back in line, dammit!
What am I going to do without you?
Want me to pack some cake for you, so you don't get hungry?
We can wait a little longer.
I knew Ingvild was using you.
I didn't even get to say goodbye.
There are more Ingvilds beyond the mountain.
We can say goodbye to her now.

Put one foot forward and the other will follow.
We have to go. Give us the password to the gate.
Don't you want to hear the rest of the song?
We have to grow nice and big.
And when we get big enough they'll come and get us.
Leave him alone, he's been rolling for a year.
They told me I could stay, so I woke up.
Hamid, while you wait, can you wash our clothes?
Yeah, and don't forget our underwear.
we waited for something beautiful to happen to us one day.
Now I found myself in this snowy nest.
Thinking about what I've lost and what I've found on the way here.
what mountains and valleys I had to cross to find you at last!

You even have your own bathroom.
You want me on the bottom?

I'm not going to let them take you.

You can't save the world, Hamid.
Tell me about your dream, Hamid.
I flew into the dark with no direction.
I saw a candle, so I flew toward the flame.
I felt the warmth on my wings and got burnt.
You don't have to be afraid.
How did you end up here in the far north?
It ran away one day, and I ran outside to catch it.
I ran from one place to another.
From one city to the next.
And from one country to the next.
And when I finally stopped and looked around, I was up here.
Now no one can separate us.

I have to clean my room.

There once was a redheaded man
who had no eyes or ears.
So he was only redheaded in a manner of speaking.
He didn't even have arms or legs.
